```{r}
                markers_vec <- as.character(markers_to_test[[x]])
                proximity_data <- ProximityScores(subset(pg_data, cell_annotation == x),
                    lazy = TRUE,
                    meta_data_columns = c("sample_alias", "condition")
                ) |>
                    filter(marker_1 == marker_2) |>
                    filter(marker_1 %in% markers_vec) |>
                    filter(marker_2 %in% markers_vec) |>
                    collect()

                res <- DifferentialProximityAnalysis(proximity_data,
                    contrast_column = "condition",
                    reference = "resting",
                    proximity_metric = "log2_ratio",
                    min_n_obs = 5
                )
                res$group <- x
                return(res)
            },
            error = function(e) {
                message(paste("Error for cell type:", x, ":", e))
                return(NULL)
            }
        )
    }) |>
    bind_rows() |>
    mutate(p_adj = p.adjust(p, method = "fdr")) # recalculate the adjusted p values after multiple testing correction for all comparisons, not only per cell type

dir.create(here::here("results/03_statistical_testing/clustering"), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Save the differential abundance analysis results to a file
saveRDS(differential_clustering_analysis, here::here("results/03_statistical_testing/clustering/differential_clustering_analysis.rds"))

# Save the differential abundance analysis results to a csv file
write.csv(differential_clustering_analysis, here::here("results/03_statistical_testing/clustering/differential_clustering_analysis.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

```


### Summarizing plots

Here we create two types of summarizing plots for the differential clustering analysis results:

1. **Volcano plot**: Shows the relationship between effect size (log fold change) and statistical significance (-log10 adjusted p-value) for each marker, faceted by cell type. This helps identify markers that are both significantly different and have large effect sizes.

2. **Heatmap**: Displays significant markers (adjusted p-value < 0.05) across all cell types, with colors representing the effect size. This provides an overview of which markers are differentially clustered in which cell types.

```{r}
#| label: statistical_testing_differential_clustering_plots

# Determine the effect size column name
effect_size_col <- "diff_median"

# Determine the feature/marker column name
feature_col <- "marker_1"

# Extract target and reference condition names from the dataframe
# Handle multiple target conditions by identifying all comparisons
reference_condition <- "resting" # Known from DifferentialProximityAnalysis call

# Determine target conditions - check if dataframe has target/contrast columns
if ("target" %in% colnames(differential_clustering_analysis)) {
    # If target column exists, get all unique targets
    target_conditions <- unique(differential_clustering_analysis$target)
    target_conditions <- target_conditions[!is.na(target_conditions)]
} else {
    stop("Could not find target column. Available columns: ", paste(colnames(differential_clustering_analysis), collapse = ", "))
}

# Prepare base data for plotting (add target column if it doesn't exist)
plot_data_base <- differential_clustering_analysis |>
    mutate(
        neg_log10_p_adj = -log10(p_adj),
        significant = p_adj < 0.05,
        effect_size = !!sym(effect_size_col),
        marker = !!sym(feature_col)
    )



# Loop through each target condition and create plots
for (target_condition in target_conditions) {
    # Filter data for this target (if target column exists)
    if ("target" %in% colnames(plot_data_base)) {
        plot_data <- plot_data_base |>
            filter(target == target_condition)
    }

    # Skip if no data for this target
    if (nrow(plot_data) == 0) {
        warning(paste("No data found for target condition:", target_condition))
        next
    }

    # Create directory name based on target and reference
    comparison_dir <- paste0(target_condition, "_vs_", reference_condition)
    base_output_dir <- here::here("results/03_statistical_testing/clustering/differential_clustering_plots", comparison_dir)

    # Plot 1: Volcano plot showing effect size vs significance, faceted by cell type
    # This plot helps identify markers that are both significantly different and have large effect sizes
    # Identify top 5 most extreme effect sizes per cell type for labeling
    top_markers_faceted <- plot_data |>
        group_by(group) |>
        slice_max(order_by = abs(effect_size), n = 5) |>
        ungroup()

    volcano_plot_differential_clustering <- plot_data |>
        ggplot(aes(x = effect_size, y = neg_log10_p_adj, color = effect_size)) +
        geom_point() +
        scale_color_gradient2(
            low = "#0066FFFF",
            mid = "#74D055FF",
            high = "#FF0000FF",
            midpoint = 0
        ) +
        geom_hline(yintercept = 0) +
        geom_vline(xintercept = 0) +
        geom_vline(xintercept = c(-1, 1), col = "grey") +
        coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1.1 * min(plot_data$effect_size, na.rm = TRUE), 1.1 * max(plot_data$effect_size, na.rm = TRUE))) +
        facet_wrap(~group, scales = "free") +
        geom_text(
            data = top_markers_faceted,
            aes(label = marker),
            hjust = -0.2,
            vjust = 0
        ) +
        theme_bw() +
        labs(
            title = paste("Differential clustering analysis: Volcano plot (", target_condition, " vs ", reference_condition, ")", sep = ""),
            subtitle = "Effect size vs statistical significance by cell type",
            x = paste("Effect size (", effect_size_col, ")", sep = ""),
            y = "-log10(adjusted p-value)",
            color = "Effect size"
        ) +
        theme(
            strip.background = element_rect(fill = "lightblue", color = "black"),
            panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
            panel.grid.major = element_blank()
        )

    # Save faceted volcano plot in the comparison directory
    save_plot(
        filename = file.path(base_output_dir, "differential_clustering_volcano"),
        plot = volcano_plot_differential_clustering,
        width = 12,
        height = 8,
        create_dir = TRUE
    )

    # Plot 2: Non-faceted volcano plots per cell type
    # Create individual folders per cell type and save non-faceted volcano plots for each cell type
    # Get unique cell types (groups)
    unique_cell_types <- unique(plot_data$group)

    # For each cell type, create a non-faceted volcano plot
    for (cell_type in unique_cell_types) {
        # Create directory for this cell type
        cell_type_dir <- file.path(base_output_dir, cell_type)
        dir.create(cell_type_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

        # Filter data for this cell type
        cell_type_plot_data <- plot_data |>
            filter(group == cell_type)

        # Create non-faceted volcano plot for this cell type
        # Identify top 5 most extreme effect sizes for labeling
        top_markers_cell_type <- cell_type_plot_data |>
            slice_max(order_by = abs(effect_size), n = 5)

        volcano_plot_cell_type <- cell_type_plot_data |>
            ggplot(aes(x = effect_size, y = neg_log10_p_adj, color = effect_size)) +
            geom_point() +
            scale_color_gradient2(
                low = "#0066FFFF",
                mid = "#74D055FF",
                high = "#FF0000FF",
                midpoint = 0
            ) +
            geom_hline(yintercept = 0) +
            geom_vline(xintercept = 0) +
            geom_vline(xintercept = c(-1, 1), col = "grey") +
            coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1.1 * min(cell_type_plot_data$effect_size, na.rm = TRUE), 1.1 * max(cell_type_plot_data$effect_size, na.rm = TRUE))) +
            geom_text(
                data = top_markers_cell_type,
                aes(label = marker),
                hjust = -0.2,
                vjust = 0
            ) +
            theme_bw() +
            labs(
                title = paste("Differential clustering analysis: Volcano plot", sep = ""),
                subtitle = paste("Cell type: ", cell_type, " (", target_condition, " vs ", reference_condition, ")", sep = ""),
                x = paste("Effect size (", effect_size_col, ")", sep = ""),
                y = "-log10(adjusted p-value)",
                color = "Effect size"
            ) +
            theme(
                plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
                plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12),
                panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
                panel.grid.major = element_blank()
            )

        # Save non-faceted volcano plot
        save_plot(
            filename = file.path(cell_type_dir, "volcano_plot"),
            plot = volcano_plot_cell_type,
            width = 8,
            height = 6,
            create_dir = FALSE # Directory already created
        )
    }

    # Plot 3: Heatmap showing significant markers across cell types
    # This provides an overview of which markers are differentially clustered in which cell types
    # Filter for significant markers and prepare data for heatmap
    significant_markers <- plot_data |>
        filter(significant) |>
        select(marker, group, effect_size) |>
        # Pivot to wide format for heatmap
        pivot_wider(names_from = group, values_from = effect_size, values_fill = 0) |>
        column_to_rownames("marker") |>
        as.matrix()

    # Only create heatmap if there are significant markers
    if (nrow(significant_markers) > 0 && ncol(significant_markers) > 0) {
        # Determine color scale breaks based on robust data range (use 1st and 99th percentiles to reduce outlier influence)
        effect_vals <- as.numeric(significant_markers)
        effect_quantiles <- quantile(effect_vals, probs = c(0.01, 0.99), na.rm = TRUE)
        max_abs_effect <- max(abs(effect_quantiles))
        breaks_range <- seq(-max_abs_effect, max_abs_effect, length.out = 101)

        heatmap_differential_clustering <- significant_markers |>
            pheatmap(
                color = PixelgenGradient(100, "BluesCherry"),
                breaks = breaks_range,
                cellwidth = 12,
                cellheight = 8,
                cluster_rows = TRUE,
                cluster_cols = TRUE,
                heatmap_legend_param = list(title = "Effect size"),
                fontsize = 8,
                fontsize_row = 6,
                fontsize_col = 8
            ) |>
            as.ggplot()

        save_plot(
            filename = file.path(base_output_dir, "differential_clustering_heatmap"),
            plot = heatmap_differential_clustering,
            width = 10,
            height = max(6, nrow(significant_markers) * 0.3),
            create_dir = TRUE
        )
    } else {
        warning(paste("No significant markers found for heatmap visualization (p_adj < 0.05) for", target_condition, "vs", reference_condition))
    }
}
```

## Differential Colocalization Analysis

Statistical test (typically Wilcoxon) between conditions

```{r}
#| label: statistical_testing_differential_colocalization


differential_colocalization_analysis <-
    names(markers_to_test) |>
    map(function(x) {
        tryCatch(
            {
                # Store markers in a variable to ensure proper evaluation in lazy query context
                # Convert to character vector to ensure proper SQL translation
                markers_vec <- as.character(markers_to_test[[x]])
                proximity_data <- ProximityScores(subset(pg_data, cell_annotation == x),
                    lazy = TRUE,
                    meta_data_columns = c("sample_alias", "condition")
                ) |>
                    filter(marker_1 %in% markers_vec) |>
                    filter(marker_2 %in% markers_vec) |>
                    collect()

                res <- DifferentialProximityAnalysis(proximity_data,
                    contrast_column = "condition",
                    reference = "resting",
                    proximity_metric = "log2_ratio",
                    min_n_obs = 5
                )
                res$group <- x
                return(res)
            },
            error = function(e) {
                message(paste("Error for cell type:", x, ":", e))
                return(NULL)
            }
        )
    }) |>
    bind_rows() |>
    mutate(p_adj = p.adjust(p, method = "fdr")) # recalculate the adjusted p values after multiple testing correction for all comparisons, not only per cell type

dir.create(here::here("results/03_statistical_testing/colocalization"), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Save the differential abundance analysis results to a file
saveRDS(differential_colocalization_analysis, here::here("results/03_statistical_testing/colocalization/differential_colocalization_analysis.rds"))

# Save the differential abundance analysis results to a csv file
write.csv(differential_colocalization_analysis, here::here("results/03_statistical_testing/colocalization/differential_colocalization_analysis.csv"), row.names = FALSE)


```

### Summarizing plots

Here we create summarizing plots for the differential colocalization analysis results:

1. **Colocalization Heatmap**: Displays the difference in medians for marker pairs, with dot size representing statistical significance (-log10 adjusted p-value). This provides an overview of which marker pairs are differentially colocalized for each cell type and condition comparison.

```{r}
#| label: statistical_testing_differential_colocalization_plot

# Extract target and reference condition names from the dataframe
reference_condition <- "resting" # Known from DifferentialProximityAnalysis call

# Determine target conditions - check if dataframe has target/contrast columns
if ("target" %in% colnames(differential_colocalization_analysis)) {
    # If target column exists, get all unique targets
    target_conditions <- unique(differential_colocalization_analysis$target)
    target_conditions <- target_conditions[!is.na(target_conditions)]
} else {
    stop("Could not find target column. Available columns: ", paste(colnames(differential_colocalization_analysis), collapse = ", "))
}

# Get unique cell types (groups)
unique_cell_types <- unique(differential_colocalization_analysis$group)
unique_cell_types <- unique_cell_types[!is.na(unique_cell_types)]

# Loop through each target condition and cell type combination
for (target_condition in target_conditions) {
    # Create directory name based on target and reference
    comparison_dir <- paste0(target_condition, "_vs_", reference_condition)
    base_output_dir <- here::here("results/03_statistical_testing/colocalization/differential_colocalization_plots", comparison_dir)

    for (cell_type in unique_cell_types) {
        # Create directory for this cell type
        cell_type_dir <- file.path(base_output_dir, cell_type)
        dir.create(cell_type_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

        # Filter data for this target and cell type
        # Set diff_median to 0 for non-significant results (p_adj >= 0.001) to focus on highly significant changes
        plot_data <- differential_colocalization_analysis %>%
            filter(group == cell_type & target == target_condition) %>%
            mutate(diff_median = case_when(
                p_adj < 0.001 ~ diff_median,
                TRUE ~ 0
            ))

        # Skip if no data for this combination
        if (nrow(plot_data) == 0) {
            warning(paste("No data found for target condition:", target_condition, "and cell type:", cell_type))
            next
        }

        # Create the colocalization heatmap plot
        # Add title indicating the target and cell type
        colocalization_heatmap <- ColocalizationHeatmap(
            plot_data,
            marker1_col = "marker_1",
            marker2_col = "marker_2",
            value_col = "diff_median",
            type = "dots",
            size_col_transform = \(x) {
                -log10(x)
            }
        ) &
            labs(size = "-log10(p_adj)") &
            scale_size(range = c(1, 5), limits = c(0, max(-log10(plot_data$p_adj)))) &
            labs(fill = "Difference\nin medians") &
            labs(
                title = paste("Differential Colocalization Analysis", sep = ""),
                subtitle = paste("Cell type: ", cell_type, " (", target_condition, " vs ", reference_condition, ")", sep = "")
            )

        # Save the plot
        save_plot(
            filename = file.path(cell_type_dir, "colocalization_heatmap"),
            plot = colocalization_heatmap,
            width = 10,
            height = 8,
            create_dir = FALSE # Directory already created
        )
    }
}
```
